title: Basic psychological need theory: Advancements, critical themes, and future directions
description: Reviews evidence that satisfaction and frustration of autonomy, competence and relatedness show distinct associations with adjustment and ill-being across life domains; autonomy means volition rather than independence.
limitations: Theory-led review by leading SDT authors; much evidence is self-report and correlational; universality remains stronger as a hypothesis than as a culture-free measurement fact.
counterevidence: Other frameworks posit additional needs or different constructs; cultural work disputes individualist readings of autonomy and measurement equivalence is incomplete.
source_title: Basic psychological need theory: Advancements, critical themes, and future directions
source_author_or_org: Vansteenkiste, Maarten; Ryan, Richard M.; Soenens, Bart
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not claim three needs exhaust flourishing, have identical expression everywhere, or that offered choice proves autonomy.

title: Student Motivation and Associated Outcomes: A Meta-Analysis From Self-Determination Theory
description: Across educational studies, more autonomous forms of motivation are generally associated with adaptive persistence, performance and well-being outcomes, while amotivation is consistently maladaptive and controlled motivation has mixed associations.
limitations: Predominantly observational studies; construct and outcome heterogeneity; educational samples are not a general theory of flourishing; publication and common-method bias remain possible.
counterevidence: Controlled motives can coexist with performance, and associations do not establish that increasing autonomous motivation alone causes all outcomes.
source_title: Student Motivation and Associated Outcomes: A Meta-Analysis From Self-Determination Theory
source_author_or_org: Howard, Joshua L.; Bureau, Julien S.; Guay, Frédéric; Chong, Jane X. Y.; Ryan, Richard M.
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not equate autonomous motivation with unrestricted choice, compliance with endorsement, or academic performance with flourishing.

title: Pathways to Student Motivation: A Meta-Analysis of Antecedents of Autonomous and Controlled Motivations
description: Need satisfaction predicts more self-determined student motivation; competence was the strongest predictor in this synthesis, followed by autonomy and relatedness, and teacher autonomy support related more strongly than parental support to the included school outcomes.
limitations: Associational pathways dominate; studies and measures are heterogeneous; relative predictor rankings are model- and setting-dependent; school outcomes are narrower than flourishing.
counterevidence: The broader SDT literature does not establish a fixed universal ranking among needs, and parent or community influence may be larger for outcomes not sampled here.
source_title: Pathways to Student Motivation: A Meta-Analysis of Antecedents of Autonomous and Controlled Motivations
source_author_or_org: Bureau, Julien S.; Howard, Joshua L.; Chong, Jane X. Y.; Guay, Frédéric
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not turn the observed predictor ranking into a universal hierarchy or infer that competence should dominate other goods.

title: The effects of choice on intrinsic motivation and related outcomes: A meta-analysis of research findings
description: Providing choice has, on average, positive effects on intrinsic motivation, effort, task performance and perceived competence, with effects depending on the type and number of choices.
limitations: Substantial heterogeneity; many short laboratory studies; choice manipulations are not equivalent to durable agency or available opportunity.
counterevidence: Choice can burden, confuse or be merely cosmetic; excessive or trivial choice does not reliably improve outcomes.
source_title: The effects of choice on intrinsic motivation and related outcomes: A meta-analysis of research findings
source_author_or_org: Patall, Erika A.; Cooper, Harris; Robinson, Jorgianne C.
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not claim more choice is always better, that choice equals autonomy, or that a menu of options establishes meaningful control.

title: Intrinsic motivation and extrinsic incentives jointly predict performance: A 40-year meta-analysis
description: Intrinsic motivation and extrinsic incentives both predict performance; intrinsic motivation is more strongly related to performance quality, while incentives are more strongly related to performance quantity, and the two can jointly operate.
limitations: Performance is not flourishing; included settings and measures vary; correlational evidence is mixed with experiments and cannot resolve every causal pathway.
counterevidence: External incentives are not uniformly harmful and may support quantity; intrinsic motivation is not sufficient for skilled performance without knowledge, opportunity and feedback.
source_title: Intrinsic motivation and extrinsic incentives jointly predict performance: A 40-year meta-analysis
source_author_or_org: Cerasoli, Christopher P.; Nicklin, Jessica M.; Ford, Michael T.
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not oppose all extrinsic motivation to well-being or infer that enjoyment alone produces competence.

title: A meta-analytic investigation of the within-person self-efficacy domain: Is self-efficacy a product of past performance or a driver of future performance?
description: Within persons, prior performance predicts later self-efficacy more strongly than self-efficacy predicts later performance once prior performance is considered, challenging simple confidence-causes-competence accounts.
limitations: Focused on self-efficacy and performance rather than broader competence need satisfaction or flourishing; designs and domains vary.
counterevidence: Between-person and experimental work can still find useful effects of efficacy beliefs under some conditions.
source_title: A meta-analytic investigation of the within-person self-efficacy domain: Is self-efficacy a product of past performance or a driver of future performance?
source_author_or_org: Sitzmann, Traci; Yeo, Gillian
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not treat confidence as competence, infer fixed ability from low confidence, or deny any causal role for efficacy beliefs.

title: Needs and subjective well-being around the world
description: Using Gallup World Poll data, fulfilment of basic needs was associated with subjective well-being across world regions; societal circumstances and individual need fulfilment made distinguishable contributions, and associations varied by well-being component.
limitations: Cross-sectional self-report; broad proxy measures; national and linguistic measurement equivalence is imperfect; association is not universality of one moral model.
counterevidence: Culture and material conditions affect expression and relative salience; unmet needs can coexist with reported positive affect or social desirability.
source_title: Needs and subjective well-being around the world
source_author_or_org: Tay, Louis; Diener, Ed
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not infer identical need expression, individual responsibility for deprivation, or that subjective well-being alone defines flourishing.

title: Differentiating autonomy from individualism and independence: A self-determination theory perspective on internalization of cultural orientations and well-being
description: Across four cultural samples, acting with a sense of volition toward both individualist and collectivist practices was associated with well-being, supporting a distinction between autonomy and independence.
limitations: Student samples, self-report and correlational design; cultural orientations were simplified; authored within SDT.
counterevidence: Relational and structural critics argue that endorsement may itself be shaped by power, adaptive preference or limited alternatives.
source_title: Differentiating autonomy from individualism and independence: A self-determination theory perspective on internalization of cultural orientations and well-being
source_author_or_org: Chirkov, Valery; Ryan, Richard M.; Kim, Youngmee; Kaplan, Ulas
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not equate autonomy with separation from family or community, or treat reported endorsement as proof that conditions are non-coercive.

title: What Schools Need to Know About Fostering School Belonging: A Meta-analysis
description: School belonging is associated with multiple individual and social factors; teacher support and positive personal characteristics were among the strongest correlates in the reviewed adolescent literature.
limitations: Terminology and measures were inconsistent; evidence was largely correlational and school-specific; publication and high-income-country bias are possible.
counterevidence: Belonging can coexist with exclusionary group norms, conformity or unsafe institutions; not all belonging is beneficial.
source_title: What Schools Need to Know About Fostering School Belonging: A Meta-analysis
source_author_or_org: Allen, Kelly-Ann; Kern, Margaret L.; Vella-Brodrick, Dianne; Hattie, John; Waters, Lea
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not individualise belonging failure, infer causality from correlates, or treat belonging as unconditional conformity.

title: Adolescents' experiences of school belonging: A qualitative meta-synthesis
description: Adolescent accounts synthesised belonging as being safe to be oneself through relationships, with identity recognition, group membership and safety recurring as mechanisms.
limitations: Only eight qualitative studies; context and researcher interpretation matter; cannot estimate prevalence or causal effect.
counterevidence: Group membership can require concealment or compliance, and safety may be uneven across identities.
source_title: Adolescents' experiences of school belonging: A qualitative meta-synthesis
source_author_or_org: Craggs, Holly; Kelly, Catherine
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not treat this synthesis as a universal definition or infer that nominal inclusion produces experienced belonging.

title: Measuring What Matters for Child Well-being and Policies
description: Child well-being requires multidimensional, child-centred measurement of current lives as well as future outcomes; important international data gaps concern children's own views, relationships, material conditions and inequalities.
limitations: OECD-country emphasis; indicator availability can drive framework content; official synthesis is not a causal meta-analysis or moral consensus.
counterevidence: Non-OECD contexts and community-specific conceptions may prioritise dimensions or indicators differently.
source_title: Measuring What Matters for Child Well-being and Policies
source_author_or_org: OECD
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not equate measurable indicators with the whole good, aggregate averages with individual experience, or future productivity with child flourishing.

title: Adolescent well-being: A definition and conceptual framework
description: WHO/UN partners propose five interconnected domains spanning health and nutrition, connectedness and contribution, safety and support, learning/competence, and agency/resilience, framed by rights and resources.
limitations: Consensus framework rather than causal review; broad domains reflect normative and policy choices; operationalisation and cross-cultural validation remain incomplete.
counterevidence: Alternative models divide or weight domains differently, and resilience language can obscure preventable adversity.
source_title: Adolescent well-being: A definition and conceptual framework
source_author_or_org: Ross, David A.; Hinton, Rachel; Melles-Brewer, Meredith; Engel, Danielle; Zeck, Willibald; Fagan, Laurel; Herat, Jane; Phaladi, Grace; Imbago-Jácome, Daniela; Anyona, Philip; et al.
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not present the five domains as empirically exhaustive, use resilience to excuse harmful conditions, or reduce rights to subjective satisfaction.

title: Well-being is more than happiness and life satisfaction: A multidimensional analysis of 21 countries
description: Across 21 countries, multidimensional psychosocial functioning added information beyond happiness and life satisfaction, and the structure and distribution of well-being differed across populations.
limitations: Convenience/online samples in several countries; measurement model choices; cross-sectional self-report; country coverage is not global.
counterevidence: Some hedonic and eudaimonic measures overlap strongly, and factor structures can shift with instruments and translations.
source_title: Well-being is more than happiness and life satisfaction: A multidimensional analysis of 21 countries
source_author_or_org: Ruggeri, Kai; Garcia-Garzon, Eduardo; Maguire, Áine; Matz, Sandra; Huppert, Felicia A.
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not equate flourishing with happiness, treat one composite score as a complete person, or assume invariant weights across cultures.

title: Different types of well-being? A cross-cultural examination of hedonic and eudaimonic well-being
description: Hedonic and eudaimonic well-being measures were highly correlated across samples, suggesting they are distinguishable but substantially overlapping rather than cleanly separate kinds of well-being.
relevance: hedonic and eudaimonic well-being; construct overlap
scope: hedonic and eudaimonic well-being; construct overlap
limitations: Cross-sectional self-report; limited cultural samples and instruments; high correlation may reflect common method or broad evaluative tone.
counterevidence: Conceptual and longitudinal work still gives reasons to distinguish feeling good from functioning, meaning and virtue.
source_title: Different types of well-being? A cross-cultural examination of hedonic and eudaimonic well-being
source_author_or_org: Disabato, David J.; Goodman, Fallon R.; Kashdan, Todd B.; Short, Jessica L.; Jarden, Aaron
method_area: hedonic and eudaimonic well-being; construct overlap
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not claim hedonia and eudaimonia are identical, or count correlated scales as proof that one moral theory is correct.

title: Eastern conceptions of happiness: Fundamental differences with Western views
description: Reviews traditions in which harmony, balance, suffering, social embeddedness and caution toward intense happiness complicate Western emphases on personal positive affect and life satisfaction.
limitations: Broad civilisational labels risk internal homogenisation; largely conceptual and interpretive rather than a systematic causal review.
counterevidence: There is large within-culture variation, cultural change and overlap; no single Eastern or Western conception exists.
source_title: Eastern conceptions of happiness: Fundamental differences with Western views
source_author_or_org: Joshanloo, Mohsen
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not essentialise cultures, romanticise suffering, or reject individual agency as merely Western.

title: Analysing wellbeing: A framework for development practice
description: Proposes well-being as an interacting process of material, relational and subjective dimensions, challenging individualised accounts and foregrounding power and social context.
relevance: relational well-being; material conditions; agency; culture
scope: relational well-being; material conditions; agency; culture
limitations: Conceptual framework informed by development practice, not a meta-analysis; categories require local empirical testing.
counterevidence: Individual subjective experience and personal projects remain important and can be obscured by overly structural accounts.
source_title: Analysing wellbeing: A framework for development practice
source_author_or_org: White, Sarah C.
method_area: relational well-being; material conditions; agency; culture
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not collapse persons into communities, presume harmonious relations, or use relationality to legitimate coercive obligation.

title: Toward an Expanded Taxonomy of Happiness: A Conceptual Analysis of 16 Distinct Forms of Mental Wellbeing
description: Argues that the hedonic/eudaimonic binary is incomplete and Western-centred, identifying additional culturally salient forms of mental well-being and warning against taxonomic closure.
limitations: Conceptual taxonomy is not evidence that all proposed forms are distinct, universal, measurable or equally valuable.
counterevidence: Parsimony and psychometric work indicate substantial overlap among many well-being constructs.
source_title: Toward an Expanded Taxonomy of Happiness: A Conceptual Analysis of 16 Distinct Forms of Mental Wellbeing
source_author_or_org: Lomas, Tim; VanderWeele, Tyler J.
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not adopt 16 forms as an empirical checklist or assume naming a culturally sourced ideal validates it for every person in that culture.

title: Rewards of kindness? A meta-analysis of the link between prosociality and well-being
description: Prosociality has a modest positive association with well-being overall, with effects varying by type of prosocial act, well-being outcome, age and other moderators.
limitations: Substantial heterogeneity; many correlational and self-report studies; benefits to actors do not establish benefits to recipients or justice of the act.
counterevidence: Caregiving burden, coerced helping, exploitation and resource costs can reduce well-being; prosocial intent can produce harmful outcomes.
source_title: Rewards of kindness? A meta-analysis of the link between prosociality and well-being
source_author_or_org: Hui, Bryant P. H.; Ng, Jacky C. K.; Berzaghi, Eleonora; Cunningham-Amos, Lucy A.; Kogan, Aleksandr
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not make contribution mandatory, infer moral worth from self-sacrifice, or claim helping always benefits helper or recipient.

title: Clarifying Eudaimonia and Psychological Functioning to Complement Evaluative and Experiential Well-Being: Why Basic Psychological Needs Should Be Measured in National Accounts of Well-Being
description: Argues that evaluative and experiential well-being omit aspects of psychological functioning and proposes basic-need satisfaction as a parsimonious complement in population accounts.
limitations: Normative-conceptual proposal by SDT scholars; measurement feasibility does not establish completeness, causal priority or political neutrality.
counterevidence: Pluralist and relational accounts identify goods, rights, virtue and material conditions not reducible to need-satisfaction reports.
source_title: Clarifying Eudaimonia and Psychological Functioning to Complement Evaluative and Experiential Well-Being: Why Basic Psychological Needs Should Be Measured in National Accounts of Well-Being
source_author_or_org: Martela, Frank; Ryan, Richard M.
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not treat a national indicator proposal as a complete definition of flourishing or as licence to optimise people to a score.

title: The Benefits of Benevolence: Basic Psychological Needs, Beneficence, and the Enhancement of Well-Being
description: Across several studies, perceived beneficent impact predicted well-being beyond autonomy, competence and relatedness, suggesting that contributing positively may add explanatory value.
limitations: Mostly self-report and short-term designs; beneficence construct overlaps with meaning and moral self-evaluation; samples do not establish universality or necessity.
counterevidence: Meta-analytic prosocial effects are modest and heterogeneous; coercion, burden and harmful helping complicate any contribution ideal.
source_title: The Benefits of Benevolence: Basic Psychological Needs, Beneficence, and the Enhancement of Well-Being
source_author_or_org: Martela, Frank; Ryan, Richard M.
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not declare beneficence a universal basic need, demand service, or infer that felt helpfulness proves actual benefit.

title: Meaning in life and physical health: Systematic review and meta-analysis
description: Meaning in life shows small-to-moderate associations with subjective physical health and health-related outcomes, with stronger and more consistent evidence for some subjective than objective indicators.
limitations: Predominantly adult and correlational evidence; construct and outcome heterogeneity; physical-health association does not define meaning's moral content or adolescent relevance.
counterevidence: Meaning can organise harmful projects, and imposed purpose or protected values can sustain coercion as well as well-being.
source_title: Meaning in life and physical health: Systematic review and meta-analysis
source_author_or_org: Czekierda, Katarzyna; Banik, Anna; Park, Crystal L.; Luszczynska, Aleksandra
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not infer one universal purpose, prescribe meaning, or claim meaningfulness guarantees moral value or objective health.

title: From loneliness to social connection: charting a path to healthier societies
description: Distinguishes connection structure, function and quality; synthesises global evidence that disconnection is common and associated with health and well-being harms, with adolescents and young adults reporting especially high loneliness.
limitations: Harmonised global estimates remain constrained by uneven country coverage, self-report, construct translation and observational evidence; the report is not adolescent-specific throughout.
counterevidence: Connection dimensions can diverge: frequent contact or large networks do not ensure felt support or relationship quality.
source_title: From loneliness to social connection: charting a path to healthier societies
source_author_or_org: World Health Organization Commission on Social Connection
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not infer a universal adolescent prevalence for every country, or that any increase in contact improves flourishing.

title: Social relationships and mortality risk: a meta-analytic review
description: Across 148 predominantly adult longitudinal studies, stronger social relationships were associated with higher survival; multidimensional social integration measures were more predictive than living arrangement alone.
limitations: Predominantly adult evidence; heterogeneous measures; observational cohorts cannot establish that relationships alone caused survival differences.
counterevidence: The weakest association was for a simple structural indicator, living alone, showing that proxies are not interchangeable.
confidence: High for adult association; Low for adolescent-specific inference
source_title: Social relationships and mortality risk: a meta-analytic review
source_author_or_org: Julianne Holt-Lunstad; Timothy B. Smith; J. Bradley Layton
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not transfer mortality effect sizes to adolescents or treat them as evidence for a specific flourishing model.

title: Rapid Systematic Review: The Impact of Social Isolation and Loneliness on the Mental Health of Children and Adolescents in the Context of COVID-19
description: Across pre-pandemic and early pandemic evidence, loneliness and social isolation were associated with later depression and anxiety in children and adolescents; duration may matter.
relevance: Child and adolescent loneliness; mental health
scope: Child and adolescent loneliness; mental health
limitations: Most included evidence was observational, heterogeneous and self-reported; the rapid-review process used partial double screening; quarantine evidence was indirect for COVID-19.
counterevidence: Isolation and loneliness are distinct, and not every period of solitude is harmful.
source_title: Rapid Systematic Review: The Impact of Social Isolation and Loneliness on the Mental Health of Children and Adolescents in the Context of COVID-19
source_author_or_org: Maria Elizabeth Loades et al.
method_area: Child and adolescent loneliness; mental health
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not diagnose mental disorder from loneliness or claim that connection alone prevents or treats depression or anxiety.

title: A meta-analytic review of the association between perceived social support and depression in childhood and adolescence
description: Across 341 studies and 273,149 participants, perceived social support had a moderate inverse association with depression; evidence favoured a general-benefits association more consistently than universal stress buffering.
limitations: Substantial construct and design heterogeneity; much evidence was cross-sectional; perceived support and depressive symptoms can influence each other.
counterevidence: Stress-buffering was not uniformly supported and source of support mattered.
confidence: High for association; Moderate for mechanism
source_title: A meta-analytic review of the association between perceived social support and depression in childhood and adolescence
source_author_or_org: Sandra Yu Rueger; Christine Kerres Malecki; Yoonsun Pyun; Chase Aycock; Samantha Coyle
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not claim all support sources are equivalent or that support causes recovery from depression.

title: Relations of friendship experiences with depressive symptoms and loneliness in childhood and adolescence: A meta-analytic review
description: Positive and negative friendship experiences show distinct associations with depressive symptoms and loneliness; relationship quality matters beyond simply having friends.
limitations: Most studies were correlational and Western; measures, ages and friendship constructs varied; directionality is not uniform.
counterevidence: Some ostensibly close friendship processes, including maladaptive co-rumination, may accompany distress rather than protect against it.
confidence: High for association; Moderate for mechanism
source_title: Relations of friendship experiences with depressive symptoms and loneliness in childhood and adolescence: A meta-analytic review
source_author_or_org: Rebecca A. Schwartz-Mette; Jessica Shankman; Aubrey R. Dueweke; Sarah Borowski; Amanda J. Rose
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not equate friendship quantity, intimacy or disclosure with uniformly beneficial connection.

title: The relationships between school belonging and students' motivational, social-emotional, behavioural, and academic outcomes in secondary education: a meta-analytic review
description: School belonging was positively associated with motivational, social-emotional, behavioural and academic outcomes in secondary students, with variation across outcomes and studies.
limitations: Predominantly correlational evidence; heterogeneous definitions and measures; school-enrolled samples and publication language constrain transportability.
counterevidence: Associations do not identify whether belonging causes outcomes, outcomes increase belonging, or both reflect school climate and prior advantage.
source_title: The relationships between school belonging and students' motivational, social-emotional, behavioural, and academic outcomes in secondary education: a meta-analytic review
source_author_or_org: Hester Korpershoek; Esther T. Canrinus; Marjon Fokkens-Bruinsma; Henk de Boer
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not treat school belonging as a unitary causal lever or substitute it for safety, inclusion or learning quality.

title: Adolescents' experiences of school belonging: a qualitative meta-synthesis
description: Adolescent accounts frame belonging through relationships, acceptance, safety, identity, participation and fit rather than mere attendance or institutional membership.
limitations: Small qualitative evidence base, context dependence and interpretive synthesis limit prevalence or causal claims.
counterevidence: Belonging can be conditional on conformity and may coexist with exclusion or pressure to suppress identity.
source_title: Adolescents' experiences of school belonging: a qualitative meta-synthesis
source_author_or_org: Holly Craggs; Catherine Kelly
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
downstream_use: Metadata retained for transparency; do not count as independent evidence.
prohibited_overextension: Do not universalise one school-belonging experience or infer that institutional identification is always healthy.

title: Impact of school-based interventions for building school belonging in adolescence: a systematic review
description: Among 22 controlled trials, 14 reported improved belonging; promising approaches often targeted strengths and positive student-student or staff-student interactions.
limitations: Few interventions intentionally targeted belonging; terminology, measures, intervention components and study quality varied; positive-study counts are not pooled effects.
counterevidence: Eight controlled trials did not report improved belonging, and the review identifies a thin, inconsistent intervention base.
source_title: Impact of school-based interventions for building school belonging in adolescence: a systematic review
source_author_or_org: Kelly-Ann Allen; Negar Jamshidi; Emily Berger; Andrea Reupert; Gerald Wurf; Fiona May
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not claim that belonging is readily engineered or that any named approach is established as effective across contexts.

title: The role of school connectedness in the prevention of youth depression and anxiety: a systematic review with youth consultation
description: Most of 34 longitudinal studies reported protective prospective associations with depression and/or anxiety, but evidence was stronger for depression; two intervention studies improved depression through differing pathways.
limitations: 69% of studies were US-based; connectedness measures varied; no diagnostic outcomes; only two intervention studies; meta-analysis was impossible.
counterevidence: Three studies were null, nine mixed, and one showed a negligible adverse association; in one intervention relationships rather than the belonging subscale mediated change.
source_title: The role of school connectedness in the prevention of youth depression and anxiety: a systematic review with youth consultation
source_author_or_org: Monika Raniti et al.
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not claim connectedness prevents clinical disorder, works identically for all groups, or has one established mechanism.

title: Toward an Integrated Model of Supportive Peer Relationships in Early Adolescence: A Systematic Review and Exploratory Meta-Analysis
description: Peer relationship quality is embedded in reciprocal interactions among identity, social-emotional functioning, family, school and wider contexts; quantity or status is not equivalent to support.
limitations: Marked construct heterogeneity, 87 measures, mostly correlational school samples, half North American, limited vulnerable populations and fair inter-rater reliability in quality appraisal.
counterevidence: Changes in number of friends or status need not indicate supportive relationships; some close processes such as dwelling in co-rumination may be unhelpful.
source_title: Toward an Integrated Model of Supportive Peer Relationships in Early Adolescence: A Systematic Review and Exploratory Meta-Analysis
source_author_or_org: Marija Mitic et al.
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not infer a settled causal model or reduce peer flourishing to popularity, network size or a single relationship skill.

title: Associations of Parenting Dimensions and Styles with Internalizing Symptoms in Children and Adolescents: A Meta-Analysis
description: Across 1,015 studies, warmth, behavioural control, autonomy granting and authoritative parenting had very small to small inverse associations with internalising symptoms; harsh and psychological control showed adverse associations.
limitations: Large heterogeneity; most effects were small; parenting and symptoms were bidirectional; broad style labels may hide culturally distinct behaviours.
counterevidence: Longitudinal evidence indicated child effects as well as parent effects; warmth alone is not a sufficient explanation.
confidence: High for small association; Moderate-Low for causal direction
source_title: Associations of Parenting Dimensions and Styles with Internalizing Symptoms in Children and Adolescents: A Meta-Analysis
source_author_or_org: Martin Pinquart
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not rank families by broad style labels or claim one parenting form causes adolescent flourishing across cultures.

title: Annual Research Review: Cross-cultural similarities and differences in parenting
description: Warmth, acceptance, control, monitoring and discipline show both cross-cultural similarities and differences; cultural normativeness and perceived legitimacy can modify meanings and outcomes.
limitations: Cross-cultural psychology still relies on imperfect construct equivalence and uneven samples; within-culture variation is substantial.
counterevidence: A behaviour labelled caring, controlling or autonomous may be experienced and predict outcomes differently across settings.
source_title: Annual Research Review: Cross-cultural similarities and differences in parenting
source_author_or_org: Jennifer E. Lansford
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not universalise Western expressions of warmth, independence, disclosure or parental authority.

title: Bidirectional Relations between Parenting and Behavior Problems from Age 8 to 13 in Nine Countries
description: Across 12 cultural groups in nine countries, parenting and child internalising/externalising problems showed bidirectional and culturally variable relations; child effects became especially salient into early adolescence.
relevance: Parent-child warmth and control; early adolescence
scope: Parent-child warmth and control; early adolescence
limitations: Non-representative city samples, self-report and measurement equivalence limits; ages 8-13 only; observational cross-lagged models do not prove causal direction.
counterevidence: Findings resist a simple parent-to-child causal story and show moderation by cultural context and developmental period.
source_title: Bidirectional Relations between Parenting and Behavior Problems from Age 8 to 13 in Nine Countries
source_author_or_org: Jennifer E. Lansford; W. Andrew Rothenberg et al.
method_area: Parent-child warmth and control; early adolescence
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not infer that parental warmth or control has one universal effect or that adolescent outcomes disclose parenting quality.

title: Testing Self-Determination Theory via Nigerian and Indian adolescents
description: Among school-aged adolescents in India and Nigeria, satisfaction of relatedness, autonomy and competence was associated with positive class evaluations and life satisfaction, supporting transport beyond Western samples.
limitations: Two countries and school samples do not establish universality; cross-sectional self-report and theory-aligned measures raise common-method and construct-equivalence concerns.
counterevidence: Support for need associations does not show that Western behavioural expressions of autonomy or relatedness are universal.
source_title: Testing Self-Determination Theory via Nigerian and Indian adolescents
source_author_or_org: Kennon M. Sheldon; Neetu Abad; Jessica Omoile
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not call relatedness an innate universal need on this evidence alone or prescribe one relational form.

title: Need Satisfaction and Well-Being: Testing Self-Determination Theory in Eight Cultures
description: Need satisfaction was associated with well-being across eight cultural samples, while strength and expression varied, providing a cross-cultural anchor for relatedness as one contributor rather than a complete model.
limitations: Mostly university/adult samples, self-report, cross-sectional design and measurement invariance constraints; not adolescent-specific.
counterevidence: Cross-cultural association does not erase culturally patterned goals, obligations or relational selves.
confidence: Moderate for cross-cultural association; Low for adolescent specificity
source_title: Need Satisfaction and Well-Being: Testing Self-Determination Theory in Eight Cultures
source_author_or_org: A. Timothy Church et al.
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not transfer adult effect estimates to KS3-KS4 learners or claim an exhaustive universal needs taxonomy.

title: Balance Across Contexts: Importance of Balanced Need Satisfaction Across Various Life Domains
description: Adolescents reporting more balanced satisfaction of autonomy, competence and relatedness across important contexts also reported higher well-being and school adjustment, suggesting that one supportive setting may not fully compensate for deprivation elsewhere.
relevance: Relatedness across home, school, friends and work
scope: Relatedness across home, school, friends and work
limitations: Self-report and largely correlational designs; composite balance metrics can be difficult to interpret; cultural and socioeconomic coverage was limited.
counterevidence: A high average can conceal one harmful context, and balance is not proof that every domain must contribute equally.
source_title: Balance Across Contexts: Importance of Balanced Need Satisfaction Across Various Life Domains
source_author_or_org: Marina Milyavskaya; Isabelle Gingras; Geneviève A. Mageau; Richard Koestner; Hugo Gagnon; Jianqun Fang; Julie Boiché
method_area: Relatedness across home, school, friends and work
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not turn contextual balance into a fixed checklist or infer causality from concurrent profiles.

title: Relationship between online social support and adolescents' mental health: A systematic review and meta-analysis
description: Online social support shows associations with adolescent mental-health outcomes, but source, platform, support type and offline context condition interpretation.
relevance: Online social support; adolescent mental health
scope: Online social support; adolescent mental health
limitations: Predominantly correlational self-report studies, platform and measure heterogeneity, regional concentration and possible reverse causation.
counterevidence: Online connection can coexist with loneliness, problematic use, exposure to hostility or displacement of desired offline support.
source_title: Relationship between online social support and adolescents' mental health: A systematic review and meta-analysis
source_author_or_org: Ziyao Zhou; Qijin Cheng
method_area: Online social support; adolescent mental health
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not equate digital contact with care, assume online support is inferior or superior, or infer platform causality.

title: Beyond Homophily: A Decade of Advances in Understanding Peer Influence Processes
description: Adolescent peer similarity arises through both selection and socialisation, with influence varying by relationship, status, behaviour and context; peer connection can transmit adaptive or maladaptive norms.
limitations: Narrative review of a methodologically heterogeneous literature; influence estimates remain vulnerable to selection confounding and network measurement limits.
counterevidence: Belonging to valued peers may increase harmful as well as prosocial behaviour, and apparent influence can partly reflect choosing similar friends.
source_title: Beyond Homophily: A Decade of Advances in Understanding Peer Influence Processes
source_author_or_org: Whitney A. Brechwald; Mitchell J. Prinstein
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not treat peers as inherently risky or protective, or infer influence from similarity alone.

title: The relationship between peer victimisation, self-esteem, and internalizing symptoms in adolescents: a systematic review and meta-analysis
description: Peer victimisation is associated with lower self-esteem and higher internalising symptoms; meta-mediation is consistent with self-esteem as one pathway but does not settle causality.
limitations: Included cross-sectional and longitudinal observational studies; self-report, heterogeneity, residual confounding and mediation assumptions limit causal inference.
counterevidence: Being embedded in a peer setting is not protective when relationships are unsafe, humiliating or exclusionary.
source_title: The relationship between peer victimisation, self-esteem, and internalizing symptoms in adolescents: a systematic review and meta-analysis
source_author_or_org: Victoria M. R. Mullan; Dennis Golm; Jacob Juhl; Sana Sajid; Valerie Brandt
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not interpret victimisation outcomes as individual deficits or claim self-esteem fully explains harm.

title: The association between perceived social support in adolescence and positive mental health outcomes in early adulthood: a prospective cohort study
description: Perceived support in adolescence, particularly friend support, predicted more positive mental-health outcomes in early adulthood after adjustment.
relevance: Adolescent support; early-adult positive mental health
scope: Adolescent support; early-adult positive mental health
limitations: Single national cohort, attrition, self-report exposure and residual confounding; perceived support may partly reflect earlier mental health or social advantage.
counterevidence: Support sources were not equivalent, and prospective association still does not prove an intervention effect.
source_title: The association between perceived social support in adolescence and positive mental health outcomes in early adulthood: a prospective cohort study
source_author_or_org: Andreas Lindegaard Jakobsen; Claus D. Hansen; Johan Hviid Andersen
method_area: Adolescent support; early-adult positive mental health
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
prohibited_overextension: Do not claim adolescent friend support guarantees adult flourishing or substitutes for family, community or structural conditions.

title: What Schools Need to Know About Fostering School Belonging: a Meta-analysis
description: Teacher support and positive interpersonal relationships were among the strongest correlates of school belonging, while individual and contextual factors also mattered.
relevance: Predictors and correlates of school belonging
scope: Predictors and correlates of school belonging
limitations: Mostly cross-sectional, heterogeneous constructs and measures, school-enrolled samples, and correlates cannot identify manipulable causes.
counterevidence: Belonging is not produced by one relationship alone; individual characteristics can reflect prior exclusion or measurement bias rather than learner deficits.
source_title: What Schools Need to Know About Fostering School Belonging: a Meta-analysis
source_author_or_org: Kelly-Ann Allen; Margaret L. Kern; Dianne Vella-Brodrick; John Hattie; Lea Waters
method_area: Predictors and correlates of school belonging
related_question: What dimensions and conditions support flourishing?
downstream_use: Metadata retained for transparency; do not count as independent evidence.
prohibited_overextension: Do not assign responsibility for belonging to adolescents or infer that teacher warmth alone can overcome unsafe or exclusionary systems.
